lever_summary: Dense i64/f64 TopK via the pass74/75 radix machinery. eval_top_k did a full per-slice comparison sort over (orig_idx, Literal, SortKey) 3-tuples then take(k). New eval_top_k_dense gathers the contiguous last-axis slice from as_i64_slice()/as_f64_slice(), stably ascending-radix the (complement total-order key, in-slice index) pairs, and takes the first k into a dense output — bypassing the SortKey/Literal machinery. The complement (!key) turns the ascending radix into descending-by-value, and stability keeps equal values in ascending index order.

alien_graveyard.primitive: radix sort/selection (non-comparison, O(n)) + dense SoA gather
alien_graveyard.mapping: reuse the integer/float total-order radix for TopK: a stable ascending radix on the complemented key yields the top-k in descending order with ascending-index ties, replacing the O(n log n) comparison sort + Literal 3-tuples

method: Same-worker comparison on vmi1153651: dense (vector_*) input vs Vec<Literal>-backed reference run in the same process (the literal input forces the generic comparison-sort path; the dense input takes the radix path).
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- topk_64k_k128 --save-baseline topk

change.lever: Added eval_top_k_dense (reuses radix_pairs_ascending + f64_total_order_key from the dense-sort passes); eval_top_k calls it first after computing output_dims/stride/n_slices, returning None (generic path) for non-dense / non-i64/f64 / short axes.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: the generic comparator is compare_sort_keys(b, a).then(a_idx.cmp(b_idx)) (descending value, ascending in-slice index for ties); a stable ascending radix on the complement key (!((v as u64)^(1<<63)) for i64, !f64_total_order_key(v) for f64) reproduces exactly that order — complement -> descending value, stability -> ascending index for equal values
isomorphism.float_semantics: f64 keys use the total_cmp bit transform, so NaN / payload-NaN / +-inf / +-0 order identically to the generic SortKey::Float (total_cmp) path
isomorphism.outputs: top-k values in input dtype (dense) + i64 in-slice indices — the same fields and order the generic path writes
isomorphism.tie_breaking: ascending in-slice index, identical to the generic .then(a.0.cmp(&b.0))
isomorphism.errors: returns None (generic path) for non-dense / non-i64/f64 storage or axes shorter than RADIX_SORT_MIN_AXIS; k>axis_size still rejected upstream

validation.name: dense_topk_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib top_k
validation.result: passed: 5 tests incl radix_top_k_dense_matches_generic (dense vs Literal/generic, i64 + f64 with NaN/+-inf/+-0/duplicate ties, multi-slice [3,800], values by bits + indices)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
